reviews.author: John Chan
reviews.text: Nice waterfalls, 30k dong roundtrip from the dropoff point to the falls via golf cart if you don't feel like walking up and down the hilly terrain. They do have hiking trails to get to the falls for those that like to hike. We paid 40k dong per person entry fee. Still good value for the money to visit

reviews.author: RONA
reviews.text: A very beautiful place! The waterfalls are stunning and the whole area looks amazing. The path down can be a bit slippery, especially after rain, so proper shoes are recommended. The descent includes many steps with a handrail. There’s also a golf cart service to take you back up (for a fee of 15,000 per person).

reviews.author: Adam
reviews.text: This is definitely worth visiting... Only 45k to visit. Safe parking at the top. A walk down and many stairs, but hand rails to keep you safe. Restaurants at the waterfall if you need. I walked back up the stairs but there is electric bus if you need. It is lovely there.

reviews.author: Johnny Kam
reviews.text: Great place to visit with fantastic waterfall. The best waterfall I've seen so far in Viet Nam. Recommend

reviews.author: Olga Lodygina
reviews.text: Good experience! But it just like sightings, you can’t swim in waterfall, just looking. Part of waterfall is closed. But still is beautiful!

reviews.author: Gautham Dakshinamurthy
reviews.text: Must visit waterfall in Danang , it’s a bit far from city - book a driver to reach here . There are a lot of coffee farm on the way - you can stop by to see the fresh coffee berries

reviews.author: Jason LKY
reviews.text: Unfortunately the place is not very well maintained, there is trash everywhere leading up to the waterfall and no proper (clean) place to even sit down, let alone have a meal.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: After seeing the incredible Ban Gioc Waterfall on the Vietnam–China border last month, I wasn’t sure anything could come close but Pongour Falls certainly gives it competition. If I were here in peak rainy season, I might even rate it equal. The falls are truly spectacular, cascading across multiple levels of volcanic rock. I honestly lost count of how many individual streams flowed into the catchment below it’s a broad, dramatic scene that’s hard to capture in just one photo. The setting feels untouched and raw, yet well-maintained enough for visitors. You can reach the base of the falls by walking down a lengthy but scenic staircase, or catch a motorbike ride for just 15,000 VND. Once down there, the panoramic view is rewarding, especially with the lush greenery surrounding the area and the thunderous sound of the water. There are warning signs not to climb or swim too close, and it’s worth respecting them the flow is strong and the rocks slippery. The viewing platforms and bridges offer safe, scenic vantage points. Pongour Falls is a must-visit if you’re in Da Lat, and even more impressive during the rainy season when the volume of water really shows off the power of nature

reviews.author: Kaiser Sause
reviews.text: beautiful place, rallye amazing but stay aware with ladies when you pay anything there or they will scam you!

reviews.author: garry killen
reviews.text: What an amazing place. Well worth the ride there on our motorcycles during our trip. We got the courtesy bus down and walked back up the steps. I'd advise doing it the other way round. There are a lot of steps back up. The falls are lovely and the valley in the gorge below them has some great views and rock formations. There were few people there which was nice.

reviews.author: Anjaneai Srivastava
reviews.text: Beautiful waterfall. Amazing view from top but even better from bottom. Don't be afraid to see how deep the walk appears, it takes not more than 10 mins to climb down the stairs and 15 mins to walk back up. You can also take bus shuttle 15k Ticket 40K/person + 5K motorcycle Cafes available at every step. Despite it is but out of way for any route, I can recommend to modify your route to add this.

reviews.author: Dmitrii Lobanov
reviews.text: Nice place. Notany tourists. There are cafes and food. Take a golf cart for ₫15k/person to go up from waterfall to the parking.

reviews.author: admen Lim
reviews.text: Entrance 40000 per person Bike to park collected 50000 per bike more expensive then human entrance Need to talk a slow walk at least 20 min to get down

reviews.author: Athena Lin
reviews.text: I was here April 9th at around 3pm and it was very warm. I was tempted to pay the 15k for a ride back to the top but took it slow and it was only a 10 minute walk back to the top. Not many people since it is a Wednesday.

reviews.author: Jan Pochobradský
reviews.text: In the middle of nowhere, there’s a breathtaking waterfall that stands out from any other we’ve seen in Vietnam. 🌿💦 Pangor Waterfall is huge, and maybe we were just lucky with the timing and season, but there were hardly any people around. 📸 This meant we could take plenty of amazing pictures without anyone in the background—definitely a rare find! The waterfall itself is absolutely beautiful, and unlike many others in Vietnam, it’s quite clean. 🧼✨ They even put effort into keeping the water under the waterfall clean, which earns them extra points from me. 👍 At the bottom, you’ll find plenty of kiosks, so there’s no need to worry about food or drinks. 🍜🥤 The entrance fee is very reasonable at 45,000 VND per person. 💰 The path down is quite steep, but if you’re not up for the walk, there’s a shuttle bus that takes you there in just two minutes for only 15,000 VND. 🚌 If you’re visiting Vietnam, this waterfall is a must-see! We’ve been here for nearly two weeks, and Pangor is definitely at the top of my list. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.author: WY
reviews.text: Beautiful water fall. Worth to pay a visit if you are at Lam Dong. Its deep inside the agricultural land. There is a electric car to take the visitors go down or go up from the hill. But need to pay 15000 Dong for single way. Or just walk down and walk up by foot. The hill Not so high. Enjoy the natural breeze.

reviews.author: isabella cheng
reviews.text: We visited as part of a tour. Beautiful, large waterfall. Easy walk down to view from the bottom. We hiked back up easily. The bridge out to the base seemed unfinished. Have to watch your steps when crossing.

reviews.author: Samudrika Gunawardane
reviews.text: Such a beautiful waterfall and a must visit if you are in Dalat.we rented out a scooter and it was 1hr from our hotel. Pack some raincoats also if any case it rains on the way or coming back. You need to buy a ticket from entrance 40000 VND and 3000VND for the parking. You can walk down to the waterfall and when coming up you can use the buggy service 15000VND per person. There are places to wat and drink near the waterfall. We captured some amazing photos.

reviews.author: Phua Kea Tong
reviews.text: Known as 7 level waterfall, cool place , Chill 😎

reviews.author: Rokas Didrikas
reviews.text: Even though it's a touristy place, but it was worth it. We came right on opening and we were completely alone. People started coming around 9pm. There are some nice boulders and short walls for climbing. You can get on the top of the waterfall throw the short forest.

reviews.author: Dan_In_Nam_2026
reviews.text: Great experience and lasting memories to go home with. First time ever I've eaten food with water flowing over my feet. Food was great and surroundings was beautiful to end a great day. Highly recommend

reviews.author: Dominic Nguyen-Kessell
reviews.text: Pongour Falls is one of the most spectacular natural wonders in Vietnam, and it's definitely worth a visit for anyone who loves nature and adventure. Located in the Central Highlands region, about 50 kilometers south of Dalat city, Pongour Falls is a breathtaking cascade of water that drops from a height of over 40 meters. The first thing that strikes you about Pongour Falls is its sheer size. The waterfall is wide and powerful, and the sound of the water crashing down is deafening. As you get closer, you'll see that the waterfall is surrounded by lush vegetation and towering cliffs, creating a stunning natural backdrop. One of the best things about Pongour Falls is that it's not overrun with tourists. While it's a popular destination, it's still relatively unknown compared to other attractions in Vietnam, so you can enjoy the beauty of the waterfall without crowds of people. There are several ways to enjoy Pongour Falls. You can take a leisurely stroll around the falls, enjoying the scenery and taking in the fresh air. You can also climb up to the top of the waterfall for a stunning panoramic view of the surrounding landscape. For the more adventurous, there are also opportunities to go swimming in the pools at the base of the falls or go on a zip-line adventure across the falls. Overall, Pongour Falls is a must-visit destination for nature lovers and adventure seekers in Vietnam. Its stunning beauty, combined with its peaceful and uncrowded atmosphere, make it a truly unforgettable experience.

reviews.author: Eric Berberich
reviews.text: It's a nice waterfall - though in February surely not full of water. I suggest to only go with an organized tour - and NOT with a taxi ... it's otherwise too pricey for what you get to see. Their is a street - that you can walk around 10min - or there is also stairs. For 15k VND you also get a shuttle service back to the top.

reviews.author: PL Lau
reviews.text: Khu Du Lịch Thác Pongour - A Must-See Natural Wonder I recently had the pleasure of visiting Khu Du Lịch Thác Pongour, and it was an absolutely incredible experience. The waterfall is simply breathtaking, cascading down a series of rocky cliffs and creating a stunning display of nature's power. The surrounding area is equally beautiful, with lush greenery and crystal-clear water. There are plenty of opportunities for adventure at Pongour Falls. You can hike to the top of the falls, swim in the natural pools, or simply relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. There are also several viewpoints where you can take in the panoramic views of the waterfall and its surroundings. I highly recommend visiting Khu Du Lịch Thác Pongour if you're ever in the Lam Dong province. It's a truly unforgettable experience that will create lasting memories.

reviews.author: Danah P.
reviews.text: It's an enchanting place. I heard from a local that it's the most beautiful waterfall in Da Lat. I went here with two other German tourists and we all enjoyed it. It brought us serenity. It's nice that it's not that crowded. You can sit in the rocks and just have some nice conversation with friends or family. I'm definitely going to visit this again next time with the love of my life.

reviews.author: Hanh M. Nguyen
reviews.text: It’s beautiful! So many big trees! Definitely a good plus there. Plus, there isn’t much of a crowd due to the pandemic! Wouldn’t think it could be as nice and enjoyable if there were so much more others there. The restrooms are a ay-nay-no!

reviews.author: John Lee
reviews.text: Incredibly beautiful fall with a short hike. There are steps you can take or just use the long ramp. If you get tired of walking for a small fee someone will bring you back to the top on a scooter.

reviews.author: Aimee Egan
reviews.text: Best waterfall in dalat skip dalanta waterfalls and make sure you come here really interesting formation of the rocks. Easy to walk down to only a 5 minute walk so don't bother with the bus down.

reviews.author: rkwphoon
reviews.text: Nice scenery. Worth a short visit. Maybe half an hour maximum. Swimming is not allowed here.

reviews.author: Alex Rolls
reviews.text: Came here on a tour of the waterfalls with our hostel and wasn't disappointed! Another waterfall to check off the list. Didn't get to swim in this one but it's a really photogenic place so I got some quality shots 📸. It did get quite busy toward the end as lots of school trips come here but still great to see an awesome natural formation.
